title: Boulder, Co Vlog 4/1/18: 2 2 hr rides, 68.44 mi, 155W, 145HR, built playlists

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on April 1, 2018, recapping a day of indoor cycling (two 2-hour rides totaling 68.44 miles at 155W average power and 145 average heart rate), explaining why he couldn't film with his video cameras (dead battery, bad memory cards), and describing side projects including building YouTube dubstep cycling playlist videos and superimposing triathlon swim course maps onto local Boulder landmarks and historical sites.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: swim course mapping / Google Earth visualization
power meter training and FTP testing

stances.position: considers his ride trainer setup the best ever made
believes testing while out of shape is valuable for future reference even if inconvenient
aware he is burning through money but embracing a carefree vacation mindset
prefers using cadence as a proxy for effort level because the wattage display fluctuates too much

entities: Augustus (Lake of Augustus / Naumachia Augusti, Rome)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I intended to sleep after my last video at 5 o'clock yesterday but ended up making bite racing videos with dubstep music for two hours instead.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I couldn't use my video cameras today because one had a card error and the other had a dead battery.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to buy new memory cards and a new battery for my video cameras.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once my video camera is working, I'll do a biweekly history test and a demo of my bicycle trainer table.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I recently discovered a one-commoner race track swim in Boulder available on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 6 to 7:30 AM.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I built a race course in Boulder back in 2013 for triathlon training.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I experimented with stacking pool laps mathematically to visualize distances, like calculating 65.6 laps as a third of a full pool.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I superimposed my pool course on top of a lake in Boulder and compared it to the Lake of Augustus in Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I duplicated and adjusted the height of pool visuals to make swimmers more visible in screenshots.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I superimposed the swim course on my neighborhood to help visualize the distance.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I spent time editing videos using Mobile TV Editor, compiling a two-hour playlist of my favorite jumps.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Mobile TV Editor can convert large video files into MP3 format quickly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I slept all day and woke up at 6:30 PM, feeling like I'm on vacation.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I bought a lot of soup and am spending money freely, as if I'm on vacation.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel like I'm staying in a magical hotel and taking my time with everything.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done my video or writing yet but plan to start soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought I wouldn't ride today because it was snowing, but I decided to ride anyway.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I averaged 140 watts on my bike and might do an hour-long test to measure my current fitness level.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe it's important to test my fitness now so I can track progress later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have a chart for pedaling with watts and plan to figure out my optimal cadence.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I talked for over 10 minutes in this video and will link my ride data later.
